data.title: Strategic Broth Acquisition Fails to Offset High Leverage and Growth Headwinds, Supporting Sell Rating on B&G Foods
data.description:
In a report released yesterday, Robert Moskow from TD Cowen maintained a Sell rating on B&G Foods, with a price target of $3.50.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Robert Moskow has given his Sell rating due to a combination of factors tied to B&G Foods’ financial profile and growth outlook. While he views the acquisition of the College Inn and Kitchen Basics broth brands as strategically sound and attractively priced, he remains concerned that it does not fully offset the company’s broader structural issues. The acquired assets add meaningful sales, EBITDA, EPS, and free cash flow, and fit well alongside B&G’s existing Bear Creek dry soup mix line, but the underlying category trends and brand performance—particularly the recent double‑digit declines at College Inn—signal that execution risk remains elevated. In his view, stabilizing these brands will require sharper pricing and more effective promotions, which could limit the margin upside from the deal.

Moskow’s continued Sell recommendation is driven primarily by B&G’s high leverage and ongoing growth headwinds. Even with the incremental earnings and cash flow from the acquisition, he believes the balance sheet risk warrants a discounted valuation versus peers. His updated target price of $3.50 per share, despite being modestly higher to reflect the benefits of the transaction, is still below the current trading level implied by the Sell rating. Overall, he concludes that the strategic positives of this acquisition are not sufficient to change the risk‑reward profile of the stock, leaving limited upside and a cautious stance for investors.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
